headline: Vibrancy Initiative — five-year context for the May 15 awards
paragraphs: Newly visible in the program-history framing: since the Vibrancy Initiative launched in 2021, it has invested in 100+ projects and leveraged $135M+ in private-sector capital into Toledo redevelopment. The 2026 cycle ($630K pool → 19 projects → est. $13.5M private spend, announced May 15) is on-trend rather than an outlier — the leverage ratio has held in the 20–25× range across cycles.
why_it_matters: The 22× leverage figure on the 2026 cycle reads better when paired with multi-year context: the city isn't claiming a one-off; it's claiming a repeatable program at scale. If you're modeling future commercial-corridor investment in Toledo, the right base case is "$600–650K of city money per cycle drawing $13–15M of private spend" — and that's the framing the Department of Economic Development is using publicly to justify the budget.
